extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won stage 5 sprint after late crash took down MEZGEC Luka and delayed URÁN Rigoberto, MOSCON retained GC lead
tactical_patterns: DILLIER Silvan swept all four KOM climbs from breakaway, securing classification before final stage—breakaway KOM focus wins jerseys.
TRENTIN positioned on Bora-Hansgrohe leadout train despite having teammate, conserving energy while rival team controlled sprint setup.
ACKERMANN Pascal's leadout caused late crash with MEZGEC but still finished second—aggressive final positioning creates collision risk.
Sky controlled 212km stage despite wet conditions to defend nine-second GC gap—long stages favor teams with depth.

rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: opportunistically used rival Bora leadout with only one teammate, opened sprint at 200m marker to win
rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: took all four KOM climbs from breakaway including category 1 with 33km remaining, locked up classification
rider_signals.MOSCON Gianni: positive: Sky controlled entire 212km stage in rain to defend narrow nine-second GC lead going into final stage
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: second place but leadout train caused crash with MEZGEC, took over points jersey despite not winning
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: neutral: breakaway workhorse adding watts but ultimately caught, predictable domestique role despite TT pedigree
rider_signals.EENKHOORN Pascal: neutral: KOM ambitions from breakaway but lost all climbs to DILLIER despite early positioning
rider_signals.URÁN Rigoberto: negative: caught in late crash, same time but delayed sprint participation—positioning error cost opportunity
